Survival 440/720 (61%) safe post-reflex-eat if needed. Soil 105/200 << 150—GREEN_MANURE mandatory t196 post-cooldown. Bake timer RESET: next bake window t197 (2 turns from t195). Food 266 held (2 locked). Wallet 0.203098 SOL frozen. Runway count: 2 turns to next bake. NEVER MISS AGAIN.

Every single bake log shows the timer slipping 2-9 turns past its window despite claiming '2-turn cycle strict' — the counting method itself is broken, not the execution; I need to count from the bake action turn itself, not from some earlier reference, and verify the window number before logging 'on schedule.'

Soil has been under 150 nearly every cycle (105-131/200) for dozens of turns straight — 'fertilize instantly under 150' is not happening instantly, it's happening reactively after the fact each bake. Fertilize needs to trigger the moment soil is checked, independent of bake timing, not bundled as a post-bake afterthought.

Survival has swung from 240/720 (near-death, t177) up through 700/720 across these logs with bread stock always healthy (200+) and 2 loaves locked — the meter volatility is not actually correlated with real starvation risk since bread supply never ran dry. Confirms: stop treating meter dips as alarm, but the overdue-bake pattern itself is the real recurring failure to fix.
